text.title: Hand-off rules between role-pinned models
text.summary: How to pass state across model boundaries without losing the signal that justified the split.

section.title: Hand-off as artifact, not chat
section.p: The hand-off between roles is a structured artifact (plan, file list, criteria checklist) — not a copy of the conversation. The cheaper model receives only what it needs to do its narrow job; the stronger model rereads the artifact when it returns to review.

rule.statement: Each role hand-off must be a structured artifact (JSON plan, list of file paths, list of acceptance criteria) — never a verbatim copy of the prior conversation; the next role consumes the artifact, not the chat.
rule.rationale: Conversation history pollutes the cheaper model with strong-model reasoning it cannot reproduce, and it bloats the stronger model when it returns to review. Artifacts keep each role's input minimal and its output verifiable.

example.note: Implementer (mid model) gets a closed envelope. Reviewer (strong model) compares result to the same envelope.

example.code: # implementer prompt "Here is everything Opus said so far. Continue."
example.note: Floods Sonnet with reasoning it cannot reproduce; budget is wasted re-reading instead of editing.

section.title: Verification at boundaries
section.p: Each role transition is also a verification gate. The stronger model returns to check that the cheaper model did the narrow job correctly; if not, request changes — do not silently repair.

rule.statement: After every transition from a stronger to a weaker model, the stronger model must verify the artifact returned by the weaker one against the original acceptance criteria before any further action.
rule.rationale: Without an explicit gate, errors compound across roles: a misclassified file path in the small model corrupts the entire execution step. The verification is cheap (the strong model only reads a small artifact) and prevents expensive cascades.

antipattern.description: Letting the weakest model both classify AND execute when classification confidence is low.
antipattern.reason: The cheap model masks its own uncertainty; without a verification gate, low-confidence classifications silently propagate into execution.

section.reference: Claude Code sub-agents — pinning model per agent.
Anthropic — model selection guidance for multi-step agents.
